text.title: Config Lives In One Place Per Language
text.summary: Ruff configuration belongs in pyproject.toml under [tool.ruff]; biome configuration belongs in biome.json at the project root — never split across files.

section.title: Co-locate config with the manifest
section.p: Splitting linter config across multiple files makes the active rule set indeterminate to both humans and agents. pyproject.toml is the single source for Python tools (PEP 621); biome.json is biome's only config and the schema is published and versioned.

rule.statement: Python projects MUST place all ruff configuration in `pyproject.toml` under the `[tool.ruff]` and `[tool.ruff.lint]` tables; the legacy files `.flake8`, `setup.cfg [flake8]`, `tox.ini [isort]`, `pyproject.toml [tool.black]`, `pyproject.toml [tool.isort]` MUST be removed during migration. JavaScript/TypeScript projects MUST place all biome configuration in `biome.json` (or `biome.jsonc`) at the project root with the `$schema` field set; `.eslintrc*`, `.prettierrc*`, and `.editorconfig` (for JS-only repos) MUST be removed.
rule.rationale: A single config file means the agent reads one place to know what rules apply. Stale `.eslintrc` next to `biome.json` confuses contributors and IDEs alike, leaks rules that biome does not honor, and breaks the determinism that the formatter's autofix loop depends on.

example.code:
# pyproject.toml — entire ruff config in one place [tool.ruff] line-length = 100 target-version = "py312"
[tool.ruff.lint] select = ["E", "W", "F", "I", "B", "C4", "UP", "SIM", "DJ", "T20"] ignore = ["E501"]
[tool.ruff.format] quote-style = "double"
example.note: One file, one schema, one source of truth.

example.code: # repo with split, partly stale Python config .flake8 # max-line-length = 88 pyproject.toml # [tool.black] line-length = 100 pyproject.toml # [tool.ruff] line-length = 120
example.note: Three line-length values active in the same repo; whichever tool runs last wins, and CI vs hooks disagree.

antipattern.description: Leaving the old `.eslintrc.js` "for the IDE" after switching the CLI/hooks to biome.
antipattern.reason: VS Code's ESLint extension still applies the old rules on save while biome rewrites them on commit; the file ping-pongs and the diff is unreviewable.
